commentary: Honored to be joining the New England Cities Initiative this summer as a Whitfield Research Fellow, digging into small-city zoning reform and displacement. Grateful to Prof. Marsh for the push to apply. More to share as the project takes shape.

Spent the week turning messy parcel-level data into something readable in R and ggplot for my Data Visualization final on urban heat and zoning overlap. Wild how much a clean choropleth changes the conversation. Always taking recs for good spatial-data resources.

Senior year and the grad-school cycle are officially underway. If you work in urban policy, municipal planning, or applied data analysis, I'd genuinely value a 20-minute coffee chat. First-gen student trying to make the most of every conversation.
Wrapped my Whitfield fellowship summer at the New England Cities Initiative — grateful for the mentorship on small-city zoning reform.
Presenting early thesis findings on zoning and displacement at the PSSA panel this week. Come say hi.
Applied to MPP programs this cycle. Onward to the next chapter in public policy.
Grateful to Prof. Marsh and Prof. Whitmore for the reference letters and the sharp feedback.
Reflecting on a semester of balancing a thesis, a barista job, and grad apps — momentum through the boring middle.
New choropleth maps for the two case cities are finally rendering. Data viz is 90% cleaning.
